preview_text:
The model rates Senegal as favorite with a 60% win probability, giving Iraq just over one chance in eight to prevail. The gap between the two outcomes is substantial enough to establish a clear hierarchy, though not so wide as to render the underdog's path implausible.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome at 27%, nearly matching Iraq's win probability. This suggests the match will likely be competitive rather than one-sided, with a realistic scenario in which neither side breaks through. The spread across outcomes—60–27–13—indicates genuine uncertainty in the middle ground but confidence in Senegal's edge.

The market consensus shifts the favorite higher, to 66%, while compressing the draw to 21%. That represents a gap of roughly 5–6 percentage points on Senegal's win line, with the market more bullish on a decisive result overall. Polymarket prices push further toward the favorite at 69%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; users can note the divergence and weigh the model's caution against the market's firmer conviction.
